pr.title: Fix LObj match being broken by someone's bugfix
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Restored sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c to Matching by reverting a prior nonmatching workaround around HSD_LightID2Index. The function return type and local index were changed from s32 to u32, the header prototype was updated to match, and the invalid GXLightID case now uses HSD_ASSERT(1170, 0) without assigning a fallback index. The PR body explains that index is intentionally uninitialized on invalid GXLightID because the code asserts, and that the previous attempted fix for the uninitialized variable made the file non-matching.
what_changed: Changed configure.py so sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c is marked Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
Changed HSD_LightID2Index return type in lobj.c from s32 to u32.
Changed the HSD_LightID2Index local index variable from s32 to u32.
Updated the HSD_LightID2Index declaration in lobj.h from s32 HSD_LightID2Index(GXLightID) to u32 HSD_LightID2Index(GXLightID).
Replaced the default invalid-GXLightID branch's direct __assert(__FILE__, 1170, "0") plus forced index = 0 workaround with HSD_ASSERT(1170, 0), leaving index unassigned in that path.
smart_moves: Recognized that the uninitialized index in the invalid GXLightID path is not a real runtime-value issue for matching purposes because the path asserts before a meaningful return.
Removed a defensive fallback assignment that satisfied source-level initialization concerns but altered code generation and broke matching.
Kept source and header prototypes synchronized when changing HSD_LightID2Index from signed to unsigned.
Used the project macro HSD_ASSERT instead of open-coded __assert, which likely better matched existing baselib style and expected generated code.
decomp_lessons: Do not add fallback assignments solely to silence or avoid uninitialized-variable concerns when the original code asserts on that path; the assignment can perturb register allocation or generated code and break matching.
For decomp matching, asserted-invalid cases may intentionally leave locals uninitialized if that reflects the original control flow.
When a function maps bitmask-like or enum-like GX identifiers to numeric indices, unsigned types may be necessary for matching even if signed types appear semantically acceptable.
Any return type correction in a C source file must be mirrored in the corresponding header to avoid prototype drift.
A file should only be moved back to Object(Matching) in configure.py after the source-level fix restores the expected object match.
naming_conventions: HSD_LightID2Index uses HSD_ prefix and CamelCase-style baselib naming.
GXLightID and GX_LIGHT0 follow Dolphin/GX type and constant naming conventions.
HSD_ASSERT(line, condition) is the preferred project-style assertion macro in this context rather than direct __assert(__FILE__, line, condition).
The header prototype in lobj.h should exactly match the implementation signature in lobj.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removing an artificial index = 0 in an assert-only default branch can restore register/lifetime behavior needed for matching.
Changing s32 to u32 for both the return type and local variable can affect generated comparisons, register treatment, or ABI-visible typing enough to matter for matching.
Avoid source changes that are logically harmless but codegen-visible in nonreachable or assertion paths.
Use configure.py Object(Matching)/Object(NonMatching) status as a visible marker of whether a translation unit currently matches.
follow_up_queries: Find other asserted-invalid default cases where locals were initialized only to avoid warnings and may break matching.
Check other baselib functions for direct __assert calls that should use HSD_ASSERT for matching or style consistency.
Search for HSD_LightID2Index callers to confirm the u32 return type is consistent with downstream expectations.
Audit configure.py entries recently changed from Matching to NonMatching due to defensive source-level bugfixes.
